domain: glaciology / glacial hydrology (englacial drainage via moulin-and-subglacial-conduit network)
specific_mechanism: Surface meltwater forms supraglacial lakes that fracture vertically into moulins (10-100 m apertures); moulin water descends through englacial conduits to the subglacial bed where it forms Röthlisberger channels that route water to the glacier terminus on hours-to-days timescales; the network is bimodal (channelized vs distributed) and reorganizes seasonally as ice flow closes channels and pressure reopens them
llm_application: A multi-stage training data routing analog: training data enters via surface 'pools' (raw corpus), high-throughput examples fracture into moulin channels (priority queues) that descend through bedded subglacial conduits (curated dataset versions) before reaching the model terminus; the network is bimodal (high-throughput channelized vs low-throughput distributed) and reorganizes seasonally (per training-epoch the channel structure rebalances as some data ages out and new data fractures in).
novelty_claim: Existing training pipelines use static data loaders or scheduled sampling. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a multi-stage moulin-and-subglacial-channel-style bimodal seasonal-reorganizing training data routing.
